title: Electroporation of Cafeteria roenbergensis Cells Using the Neon Transfection System
author: Sarah Duponchel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qvhdw36

steps.title: Before the experiment (it can be...
steps.instruction: Before the experiment (it can be the day before without affecting too much the cell viability) Density-based removal of bacteria from a culture of the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis by Sarah Duponchel

steps.title: Centrifuge the Cafeteria cells at 4,500...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the Cafeteria cells at 4,500 g, 5 min, room temperature

steps.title: Wash twice the cell pellet with...
steps.instruction: Wash twice the cell pellet with 1X PBS => use small volume of PBS to concentrate the cells

steps.title: Prepare the different reagents (CaCl 2...
steps.instruction: Prepare the different reagents (CaCl 2 and 2X HEBS) according to the followed protocol from Pia Elustondo Calcium phosphate transfection of Oxyrrhis marina with an Alexa488-labelled DNA fragment by Pia Elustondo Aliquot and autoclave the 2X HEBS buffer to avoid contamination

steps.title: Mix the CaCl 2 (final concentration...
steps.instruction: Mix the CaCl 2 (final concentration 125 mM) with the material to electroporate - for DNA electroporation complete to the final volume with water - for protein electroporation complete to the final volume with 1X PBS or the buffer the most suitable for the protein For one electroporation, final volume of the CaCl 2 mixture 10-μl tips: 3.5 μl 100-μl tips: 48.5 μl

steps.title: Add the CaCl 2 mixture onto...
steps.instruction: Add the CaCl 2 mixture onto the 2X HEBS For one electroporation, 2X HEBS volume 10-μl tips: 5 μl 100-μl tips: 50 μl Option 1: volumes are big enough to vortex constantly the HEBS while the CaCl 2 mixture is added drop by drop Option2: 1/10th of the CaCl 2 mixture is added at a time with a 3-secondes vortexing-step between each addition

steps.title: Count the cells
steps.instruction: Count the cells: stain 10 µL of Cafeteria culture with 1 µL of Lugol’s acid iodine solution and load them in a haemocytometer (Neubauer Chamber) Maximum volume of the desirable number of cells needed for the electroporation = 1.5 μl

steps.title: Add the 1
steps.instruction: Add the 1.5 μl volume of the appropriate number of C. roenbergensis cells to the CaCl 2 /HEBS mixture

steps.title: Electroporate the cells at 1600V, for...
steps.instruction: Electroporate the cells at 1600V, for 20 ms, 1 pulse using either the 10-μl or 100-μl tips

steps.title: Place the electroporated cells into 10...
steps.instruction: Place the electroporated cells into 10 time more f/2 media than the electroporated volume for 30 min

steps.title: Cells can either be fixed or...
steps.instruction: Cells can either be fixed or maintained in culture by adding 5 time fresh f/2 media complement with 0.02% yeast extract and 1/500th of E.coli culture Fluorescence microscopy with the marine heterotrophic flagellate Cafeteria roenbergensis by Monica Berjon-Otero
